body.title: Provenance: verify source_token at write boundary in cortex.py before storing
body.result: Already implemented: cortex.py store() calls verify_source_token() at lines 1576-1587 — invalid token is nullified and logged PROVENANCE_TOKEN_INVALID before the DB write proceeds
body.description:
HIGH gap found in T-murderbot-security-review.

cortex.py store() writes memory.source_token directly from the MemoryNode object without calling ProvenanceService.verify(). A prompt-injected agent can direct Igor to store memories with a fabricated source_token value -- the attribution mechanism accepts whatever it is given. This poisons trust-tier scores: forged tier_1-matching tokens could masquerade as CC-written memories.

Verified in code: grep of cortex.py lines 1533-1640 shows ensure_provenance() stamps metadata but there is no verify() call on the source_token before the INSERT.

Fix: before inserting, if source_token is non-null, call ProvenanceService.verify(source_token). If verification fails, either reject the write (strict mode) or nullify the token and log a warning (permissive mode). Permissive is the right default so existing writes are not broken.

**Affected files:** devices/igor/memory/cortex.py (store() method around line 1533)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; ProvenanceService must be accessible from cortex (inject or import)
**Scope boundary:** IN -- verify source_token in cortex.py store() before INSERT; OUT -- retroactive backfill, memory_writer.py (Librarian path; source_agent is lower risk, ticketed separately if needed)
**Completion criteria:** storing a memory with a fabricated source_token results in the token being nullified and a warning logged; valid token passes through unchanged; test covers both paths
